snp5.17:1.1: I’m old & weak, my complexion dull,
snp5.17:1.3: I’ve blurry eyes and trouble hearing—
snp5.17:1.4: but may I not perish while still deluded, confused!
snp5.17:1.5: Teach me the Dhamma so that I may know
snp5.17:1.6: the abandoning here of birth & aging.
snp5.17:2.1:
The Buddha:
Seeing people suffering on account of their bodies—
snp5.17:2.3: heedless people are afflicted on account of their bodies—
snp5.17:2.5:
let go of the body for the sake of no further becoming.
Piṅgiya:
snp5.17:3.1: In the four cardinal directions, the four intermediate,
snp5.17:3.3: there is nothing in the world unseen, unheard, unsensed, uncognized by you.
snp5.17:3.5: Teach me the Dhamma so that I may know
snp5.17:3.6: the abandoning here of birth & aging.
snp5.17:4.1:
The Buddha:
Seeing people, victims of craving—
snp5.17:4.5: let go of craving for the sake of no further becoming.
